title: 10 best herbal tinctures for asthma
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for asthma are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs that are used to alleviate asthma symptoms. These tinctures are made by soaking plant material in a mixture of water and alcohol for several weeks, then straining out the solid material. Some examples of herbal tinctures that are commonly used to treat asthma include licorice root, which helps to reduce inflammation in the airways, and lobelia, which can help to relax the muscles in the airways and improve breathing. Another example is butterbur, which has been shown to reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. By using these herbal tinctures, asthma sufferers may be able to reduce their reliance on traditional asthma medications and improve their overall quality of life. For instance, a person with asthma who uses lobelia tincture may find that they are able to breathe more easily and participate in physical activities that they were previously unable to do due to their asthma symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with asthma because it contains anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties that can help alleviate asthma symptoms. The herb's active compounds, such as lactones and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation in the airways, thereby decreasing the likelihood of asthma attacks. Moreover, yarrow tincture can help relax the muscles around the airways, making it easier to breathe. Its expectorant properties also aid in clearing mucus from the respiratory tract, further alleviating congestion and discomfort. As a result, yarrow tincture can be a valuable natural remedy for individuals seeking asthma relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids, helps reduce airway inflammation, a key aspect of asthma.
Bronchodilator: The bronchodilator effect of yarrow tincture, attributed to lactones like costunolide, aids in relaxing bronchial muscles, alleviating asthma symptoms.
Antispasmodic: Yarrow tincture's antispasmodic property, owing to alkaloids, assists in relieving spasms in the respiratory tract, minimizing asthma attacks.
Expectorant: The expectorant property of yarrow tincture, resulting from its volatile oils, helps clear mucus from the respiratory tract, improving asthma-related congestion.
Antioxidant: Yarrow tincture's antioxidant property, attributed to phenolic acids, aids in protecting lung tissue from oxidative stress, supporting overall lung health in asthma management.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Yarrow flowers are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them useful for reducing airway inflammation in asthma.
Leaves: The leaves of yarrow contain volatile oils and flavonoids that help relax bronchial spasms and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds are a source of essential fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve lung function in individuals with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried yarrow flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Chop 200g of plant material into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Pour 400ml of high-proof vodka over the plant material, ensuring all parts are fully submerged, then seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with asthma because it contains mucilage, a substance that forms a slippery film when it comes into contact with water. This film can soothe and protect inflamed mucous membranes, including those in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, marshmallow tincture has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways and thin out mucus, making it easier to expel. Overall, marshmallow tincture can provide relief for asthma symptoms by soothing, protecting, and reducing inflammation in the airways.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tincture contains mucilage that helps reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and alleviating asthma symptoms.
Expectorant: The demulcent properties of marshmallow tincture help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, reducing congestion and discomfort associated with asthma.
Soothing: The mucilage in marshmallow tincture forms a protective layer over mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation and coughing fits in asthma patients.
Immune-boosting: Rich in antioxidants, marshmallow tincture supports the immune system, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks.
Antispasmodic: Marshmallow tincture contains compounds that help relax smooth muscles, reducing bronchial spasms and alleviating asthma-related breathing difficulties.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, which can help soothe inflamed tissue in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of the marshmallow plant also contain mucilage, which can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and relieve asthma.
Flowers: The flowers of the marshmallow plant contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce asthma symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the marshmallow plant contain antioxidants, which can help protect the lungs from damage and reduce the severity of asthma attacks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated ensuring freshness and potency.
Cleanly chop the fresh plant material into small pieces and combine with vodka at a ratio of 1:2 for optimal extraction.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar for 6-8 weeks at room temperature allowing solvents to extract active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids and transfer the tincture into dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years after opening and shake well before use to ensure optimal efficacy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden angelica tincture helps with asthma because it contains anti-inflammatory and bronchodilator properties that can help alleviate asthma symptoms. The herb's active compounds, such as coumarins and furanocoumarins, work to relax the muscles in the airways, reducing inflammation and improving breathing. Additionally, angelica has been shown to stimulate the production of surfactant in the lungs, which helps to keep the airways open and clear. By promoting respiratory health and reducing inflammation, garden angelica tincture can be a valuable addition to an asthma treatment plan. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before adding any new supplements to a treatment regimen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Garden angelica tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe for those with asthma. This is due to the presence of compounds like ligustilide and butylidenephthalide.

Bronchodilator: The bronchodilator effect of garden angelica tincture helps widen the airways, alleviating asthma symptoms by improving airflow to the lungs. This is attributed to the presence of essential oils like α- and β-phellandrene.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic properties of garden angelica tincture help relax the smooth muscles in the airways, reducing constriction and improving breathing for asthma sufferers. This is due to the presence of coumarins and lactones.

Expectorant: Garden angelica tincture's expectorant properties help clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe for those with asthma. This is attributed to the presence of volatile oils and polysaccharides.

Immunomodulatory: The immunomodulatory properties of garden angelica tincture help regulate the immune response, reducing the severity of asthma attacks. This is due to the presence of polysaccharides and coumarins.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce asthma symptoms.
Seeds: Rich in essential oils with bronchodilatory properties, helping to open up airways in the lungs.
Leaves: High in flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to have antispasmodic effects on bronchial smooth muscles.
Stems: Contain coumarins, which have been suggested to help relax bronchial smooth muscles, potentially alleviating asthma symptoms.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils, providing anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, useful for managing asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature angelica archangelica roots and leaves in early morning when dew is still present.
Chop fresh or dried plant material into small pieces using scissors or a herb cutter for optimal extraction.
Combine 100g of chopped plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate tincture formation and solvent interaction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container and discard solids after 2 weeks of steeping.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with asthma because it contains thujone, a compound that acts as a bronchodilator, relaxing the muscles in the airways and making it easier to breathe. Additionally, wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the airways, further alleviating asthma symptoms. Furthermore, wormwood has been shown to have expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract. All of these factors make wormwood tincture a valuable tool in managing asthma symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wormwood tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce airway inflammation, a key factor in asthma, due to the presence of compounds like sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids.
Bronchodilator: Wormwood tincture can help dilate bronchial tubes, improving breathing for asthma sufferers, thanks to the active constituent 1,8-cineole.
Antispasmodic: Wormwood tincture's antispasmodic properties alleviate bronchial spasms, reducing asthma symptoms, by containing compounds like chamazulene and bisabolol.
Immune-modulating: Wormwood tincture's ability to modulate the immune response helps control asthma-related inflammation, as it contains various polyphenols and terpenoids.

Antioxidant: Wormwood tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect lung tissue from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall respiratory health, due to its richness in phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, wormwood leaves are used to make tinctures that help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating asthma symptoms.
Flowers: High in medicinal constituents, wormwood flowers produce tinctures that have a relaxing effect on bronchial muscles, making it easier to breathe for asthma patients.
Seeds: Wormwood seeds contain essential oils and other active compounds, which, when used in tinctures, can help dilate constricted airways in asthma sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and stems cut them into small pieces.
Combine 250 grams of chopped wormwood with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks allowing the mixture to steep undisturbed.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store them in a refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit or below.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tincture helps with asthma because it contains anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that can alleviate asthma symptoms. The herb's active compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, work to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, wild indigo tincture can help eliminate mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, providing relief from congestion and coughing. Its immune-boosting properties can also help prevent asthma attacks by strengthening the body's defenses against allergens and irritants. Overall, wild indigo tincture can be a natural and effective way to manage asthma symptoms and improve respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild indigo tincture can reduce inflammation in the airways, helping to alleviate asthma symptoms, due to its active constituents, including indigo and indirubin.
Immune-boosting: It can strengthen the immune system, helping the body to fight off respiratory infections that can trigger asthma attacks, thanks to the presence of alkaloids and polysaccharides.
Antispasmodic: Wild indigo tincture can help to relax the smooth muscles in the airways, reducing the severity of asthma attacks, due to the presence of isoquinoline alkaloids.
Antimicrobial: It can help to eliminate harmful bacteria and viruses that can exacerbate asthma symptoms, thanks to the active constituents, such as indigo and indirubin.
Expectorant: Wild indigo tincture can help to clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe, due to its active constituents, including indigo and indirubin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that help relax bronchial muscles, making it easier to breathe.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce airway inflammation in asthma.
Leaves: High in rotenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the release of histamines and leukotrienes, reducing asthma symptoms.
Seeds: Contain essential oils with antispasmodic properties, helping to alleviate bronchial spasms in asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried baptisia tinctoria leaves stems and flowers for optimal potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped baptisia tinctoria with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking daily to extract compounds.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean amber glass bottle for long-term storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bryonia tincture helps with asthma because it contains the active ingredient Bryonia alba, which is a powerful anti-inflammatory and expectorant. This herb works by reducing inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, Bryonia alba helps to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs, providing relief from congestion and coughing. Its astringent properties also help to tighten and strengthen the walls of the respiratory tract, reducing the likelihood of asthma attacks. Overall, Bryonia tincture is an effective natural remedy for asthma due to its ability to reduce inflammation, loosen mucus, and strengthen the respiratory tract.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bryonia tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe in asthma. This is due to the presence of cucurbitacins.
Bronchodilator: Bryonia tincture helps widen the airways, improving airflow to the lungs, which is crucial in managing asthma symptoms. The active constituents responsible for this are the alkaloids.
Expectorant: It aids in the expulsion of mucus from the respiratory tract, clearing the airways and reducing asthma symptoms. The saponins in Bryonia tincture provide this expectorant effect.
Antispasmodic: Bryonia tincture helps relax smooth muscles, reducing spasms in the airways, a key feature of asthma. This is attributed to the tincture's scopoletin content.
Immune-modulating: Bryonia tincture can help regulate the immune response, reducing the frequency and severity of asthma attacks. This is due to the presence of various polysaccharides.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bryonia roots are commonly used in medicinal tinctures for asthma due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the airways and clear mucus.
Seeds: The seeds of the Bryonia plant contain alkaloids that have a bronchodilatory effect, making them useful in tinctures for treating asthma symptoms such as constricted airways.
Leaves: Bryonia leaves are often used in tinctures for asthma due to their antispasmodic properties, which help to relieve muscle spasms in the respiratory tract and alleviate coughing.

Flowers: The flowers of the Bryonia plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat asthma due to their calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress associated with asthma attacks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried bryonia alba root powder weighing 100 grams.
Combine the bryonia powder with vodka 50% abv at a ratio of 1:3 parts herb to solvent.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar and steep for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place.
After steeping period strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container.
Transfer the filtered tincture to amber glass bottles and label with ingredients dosages and preparation instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tabasco tincture helps with asthma because it contains capsaicin, a compound found in chili peppers that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. Asthma is often caused by inflammation in the airways, which can lead to difficulty breathing, coughing, and other symptoms. By reducing inflammation, tabasco tincture can help to open up the airways and make it easier to breathe. Additionally, capsaicin has been shown to help relax the muscles in the airways, further reducing the symptoms of asthma. While more research is needed to fully understand the benefits of tabasco tincture for asthma, many people find that it provides relief from their symptoms and can be used as a natural alternative to traditional asthma medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Tabasco tincture's active ingredient, capsaicin, helps reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating asthma symptoms.
Bronchodilator: Capsaicin in Tabasco tincture can help dilate the airways, making it easier for asthma sufferers to breathe.
Pain relief: Tabasco tincture has analgesic properties that can help alleviate the chest pain and discomfort associated with asthma.
Mucus reduction: The active constituents in Tabasco tincture can help thin and reduce mucus production, making it easier to expel mucus and clear airways.
Immune system support: Tabasco tincture contains vitamins and antioxidants that can help boost the immune system, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of asthma attacks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain compounds that can help relax the airways, making it easier to breathe for those with asthma.
Seeds: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation and mucus production in the airways.
Flowers: Contain essential oils that can help open up the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Stems: May contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Roots: Historically used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions, including asthma, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality capsicum frutescens peppers in their ripest state for optimal flavor and potency.
Wash the harvested peppers thoroughly with filtered water to remove any impurities or debris.
Dry the washed peppers using a food dehydrator set at 135°f (57°c) for 6-8 hours to preserve their active compounds.
Combine the dried peppers with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the peppers are completely submerged in the solvent.
Store the jar in a dark, cool place and allow it to macerate for at least 30 days or up to 60 days for maximum extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Autumn crocus tincture helps with asthma because it contains a compound called colchicine, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Asthma is characterized by inflammation and swelling of the airways, leading to difficulty breathing. Colchicine helps reduce this inflammation, making it easier for individuals with asthma to breathe. Additionally, the herb has been shown to relax bronchial smooth muscles, further alleviating asthma symptoms. It is important to note that while autumn crocus tincture can be a helpful addition to an asthma treatment plan, it should not be used as a substitute for prescription medications or inhalers, and individuals should consult with their healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory property of autumn crocus tincture helps reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating asthma symptoms. This is due to the presence of the active constituent, colchicine.
Bronchodilator: The bronchodilator property of autumn crocus tincture helps widen the airways, making it easier to breathe. This is attributed to the presence of the alkaloid, methylamine.

Immune modulator: The immune modulator property of autumn crocus tincture helps regulate the immune response, reducing the likelihood of asthma attacks. This is due to the presence of the glycoside, timosaponin.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic property of autumn crocus tincture helps relax muscles in the airways, reducing the severity of asthma symptoms. This is due to the presence of the alkaloid, colchamine.
Expectorant: The expectorant property of autumn crocus tincture helps clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. This is due to the presence of the saponin, crocin.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain the highest concentration of medicinal compounds, making them ideal for treating asthma symptoms.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways.
Seeds: Contain essential oils and alkaloids, which can help relax bronchial spasms and improve breathing in asthma patients.
Leaves: Packed with flavonoids and tannins, promoting respiratory health and alleviating asthma-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh colchicum autumnale leaves and flowers on a dry sunny morning.
Clean and chop the plant material into small pieces, ensuring no visible dirt or debris remains.
Combine 50 grams of chopped plant material with 200 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for maceration, shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the plant material and preserving the resulting tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foxglove tincture helps with asthma because it contains digoxin and other medicinal compounds that can help relax bronchial muscles, decrease inflammation, and increase the efficiency of the heart, which can all contribute to improved breathing for individuals with asthma. The herb has been used traditionally to treat respiratory conditions and its active components have been found to have a positive effect on the respiratory system, making it a potentially useful natural remedy for managing asthma symptoms. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Foxglove tincture can reduce inflammation in the airways, helping to alleviate asthma symptoms. This property is important for asthma because inflammation can constrict the airways and make it difficult to breathe. The active constituents responsible for this property include glycosides such as digitoxin and digoxin.

Bronchodilatory: Foxglove tincture can help to widen the airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. This property is important for asthma because narrowed airways can trigger asthma attacks. The active constituents responsible for this property include glycosides such as digitoxin and digoxin.

Cardiotonic: Foxglove tincture can help to strengthen and regulate the heartbeat, which can be beneficial for people with asthma. This property is important for asthma because asthma can put a strain on the heart, particularly during an asthma attack. The active constituents responsible for this property include glycosides such as digitoxin and digoxin.

Expectorant: Foxglove tincture can help to loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe. This property is important for asthma because mucus can build up in the airways and make it difficult to breathe. The active constituents responsible for this property include saponins such as hederagenin and oleanolic acid.

Antispasmodic: Foxglove tincture can help to relax the smooth muscles in the airways, reducing the likelihood of asthma attacks. This property is important for asthma because spasms in the airways can trigger asthma attacks. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids such as scopolamine and atropine.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, foxglove flowers are used to help relax bronchial muscles and alleviate asthma symptoms.
Leaves: Containing cardiac glycosides, foxglove leaves can be used to make medicinal tinctures that help regulate heart function and improve breathing in asthma patients.
Seeds: High in medicinal compounds, foxglove seeds can be used to create tinctures that help dilate bronchial tubes and reduce inflammation in the airways of asthma sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest digitalis purpurea flowers at peak bloom in morning dew using scissors.
Dry the fresh flowers by spreading them on paper bags or clean cloth for 2-3 weeks.
Grind dried digitalis purpurea flowers into fine powder using mortar and pestle with a 1:4 flower-to-alcohol ratio.
Steep the powdered foxglove in 80% to 100% ethanol or vodka at room temperature for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or coffee filter, discard solids and transfer liquid tincture into dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ma huang tincture helps with asthma because it contains ephedrine, a compound that acts as a bronchodilator, relaxing muscles in the airways and making it easier to breathe. This can help alleviate the symptoms of asthma, such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. Additionally, ma huang has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the airways and further improve breathing. It's important to note that ma huang should only be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional, as it can have serious side effects if not used properly.
remedy_properties: Bronchodilation: Ma Huang tincture contains ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which help relax bronchial smooth muscles, making it easier to breathe in cases of asthma.
Anti-inflammatory: The active constituent, hesperidin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate inflammation in the airways, reducing asthma symptoms.
Expectorant: Ma Huang tincture contains tannins, which have an astringent effect, helping clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, making it easier for asthma patients to breathe.
Immunomodulation: The herb regulates immune response, reducing the likelihood of asthma attacks triggered by allergic reactions or immune system overreactions.
Vasoconstriction: Ephedrine and pseudoephedrine in Ma Huang cause vasoconstriction, reducing swelling in the airways and facilitating breathing for asthma patients.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of ma huang contain high levels of alkaloids, which help to relax bronchial smooth muscles and improve breathing in individuals with asthma.
Stems: Ma huang stems have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce airway inflammation and improve lung function in people with asthma.
Leaves: The leaves of ma huang contain ephedrine, a compound that can help dilate bronchial passages, making it easier for asthma sufferers to breathe.
Flowers: Ma huang flowers are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the lungs of people with asthma.
Seeds: Ma huang seeds contain essential fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce airway inflammation in people with asthma.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ephedra sinica leaves and stems, ensuring they are free from contaminants.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or shade drying method for 24 hours.
Grind the dried ephedra sinica into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part of the powdered ephedra sinica with three parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, allowing it to macerate for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dark place.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat asthma with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and bronchodilator herbs. Tinctures made from licorice root, marshmallow root, and butterbur extract can reduce inflammation in the airways, while lobelia and ginger tinctures can help relax the smooth muscles around the airways, making breathing easier. It's important to consult with a qualified herbalist or healthcare provider to determine the appropriate dosage and combination of herbs for your specific needs. Always follow their instructions carefully and never discontinue prescribed medications without consulting your healthcare provider.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to asthma that are treated with herbal tinctures include chronic bronchitis and COPD (Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease). Herbal tinctures such as licorice, coltsfoot, and grindelia can help reduce inflammation, relax bronchial spasms, and thin mucus, making it easier to expel. Thyme and oregano tinctures have antimicrobial properties that can help fight respiratory infections. However, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any herbal treatment to ensure safety and effectiveness.
